_**User (2026-06-24 20:23:53Z)**_

Right now `specstory resume` supports an optional 3rd argument, the target agent for the resume.

But it doesn't actually work as intended. Right now it just acts as a default... it still asks the user what agent to resume in. It should not if they provided that argument.

Also... need to make sure that it validates that optional 3rd argument. If someone provides it, and it's not a valid provider name it should error the run.

```text
171	// launchResume reconstructs (cross-agent) or natively resumes the planned session and
172	// runs the agent with auto-save + provenance — the shared tail of `resume` and `search`.
173	func launchResume(plan *resumePlan, cwd string, o resumeLaunchOpts) error {
174		// Setup output configuration and project identity (needed for auto-save + cloud).
175		outConfig, err := utils.SetupOutputConfig(o.outputDir, o.flagDebugDir)
176		if err != nil {
177			return err
178		}
179		if err := utils.EnsureHistoryDirectoryExists(outConfig); err != nil {
180			return err
181		}
182		if _, err := utils.NewProjectIdentityManager(cwd).EnsureProjectIdentity(); err != nil {
183			slog.Error("Failed to ensure project identity", "error", err)
184		}
185	
186		CheckAndWarnAuthentication(o.noCloudSync)
187		if o.onlyCloudSync && !cloud.IsAuthenticated() {
188			return utils.ValidationError{Message: "--only-cloud-sync requires authentication. Please run 'specstory login' first"}
189		}
190	
191		analytics.SetAgentProviders([]string{plan.to.Name()})
192		analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventResumeActivated, analytics.Properties{
193			"from_provider": plan.fromID,
194			"to_provider":   plan.toID,
195			"cross_agent":   plan.fromID != plan.toID,
196		})
197	
198		resumeSessionID, err := prepareResumeTarget(plan, cwd, os.Stdout)
199		if err != nil {
200			return err
201		}
202	
203		// Context for graceful cancellation (Ctrl+C).
204		ctx, cancel := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)
205		defer cancel()
206	
207		// Provenance infrastructure (mirrors run/watch).
208		provenanceEngine, provenanceCleanup, err := provenance.StartEngine(o.provenanceEnabled)
209		if err != nil {
210			return err
211		}
212		defer provenanceCleanup()
213		fsCleanup, err := provenance.StartFSWatcher(ctx, provenanceEngine, cwd)
214		if err != nil {
215			return err
216		}
217		defer fsCleanup()
218	
219		// Auto-save callback: identical behavior to run/watch.
220		sessionCallback := func(sess *spi.AgentChatSession) {
221			if sess == nil {
222				return
223			}
224			_, perr := session.ProcessSingleSession(context.Background(), sess, outConfig, session.ProcessingOptions{
225				OnlyCloudSync:      o.onlyCloudSync,
226				IsAutosave:         true,
227				DebugRaw:           o.debugRaw,
228				UseUTC:             o.useUTC,
229				NoTelemetryPrompts: o.noTelemetryPrompts,
230			})
231			if perr != nil {
232				slog.Error("Failed to process session update", "sessionId", sess.SessionID, "error", perr)
233			}
234			provenance.ProcessEvents(ctx, provenanceEngine, sess)
235		}
236	
237		slog.Info("Launching resume", "provider", plan.to.Name(), "resumeSessionID", resumeSessionID)
238		if err := plan.to.ExecAgentAndWatch(cwd, "", resumeSessionID, o.debugRaw, sessionCallback); err != nil {
239			slog.Error("Agent resume failed", "provider", plan.to.Name(), "error", err)
240			return err
241		}
242		return nil
243	}
244	
245	// prepareResumeTarget produces the native session ID to hand to ExecAgentAndWatch.
246	// Same-agent resume reuses the existing session; cross-agent reconstructs the
247	// source SessionData into the target's native store and returns the fresh ID.
248	func prepareResumeTarget(plan *resumePlan, cwd string, out io.Writer) (string, error) {
249		// Same agent: native resume on the existing session, no transform.
250		if plan.fromID == plan.toID {
251			fprintf(out, "\nResuming %s session %s in place...\n", plan.from.Name(), shortID(plan.sessionID))
252			return plan.sessionID, nil
253		}
254	
255		// Cross-agent: load source SessionData, reconstruct, write into target store.
256		// The source must be read from the directory it was originally launched in, not the
257		// user's current cwd — a session picked from another project (all-projects browser)
258		// lives elsewhere, and providers like Codex locate it by its recorded cwd. The
259		// reconstructed session is still written and launched under the current cwd below.
260		fromCwd := plan.fromCwd
261		if fromCwd == "" {
262			fromCwd = cwd
263		}
264		fromSession, err := plan.from.GetAgentChatSession(fromCwd, plan.sessionID, false)
265		if err != nil {
266			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to load source session: %w", err)
267		}
268		if fromSession == nil || fromSession.SessionData == nil {
269			return "", fmt.Errorf("source session %s has no data to reconstruct", plan.sessionID)
270		}
271	
272		note := fmt.Sprintf("Resumed from a %s session via SpecStory.", plan.from.Name())
273		rec, err := plan.to.ReconstructSession(fromSession.SessionData, spi.ReconstructOptions{
274			WorkspaceRoot: cwd,
275			MigrationNote: note,
276		})
277		if err != nil {
278			if errors.Is(err, spi.ErrReconstructionUnsupported) {
279				return "", utils.ValidationError{Message: fmt.Sprintf(
280					"%s can't yet be a cross-agent resume target. Choose Claude Code or Codex CLI (or resume in %s itself).",
281					plan.to.Name(), plan.from.Name())}
282			}
283			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to reconstruct session for %s: %w", plan.to.Name(), err)
284		}
285	
286		path, err := plan.to.NativeSessionPath(cwd, rec.Filename)
287		if err != nil {
288			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to resolve target session path: %w", err)
289		}
290		if err := writeReconstructedSession(path, rec.Content); err != nil {
291			return "", err
292		}
293		// The agent runs in a separate process and resolves the session by reading this file the
294		// instant it starts. On a filesystem without strong close-to-open coherence the freshly
295		// written file can lag, so confirm it is actually readable before handing the agent a
296		// --resume it would otherwise reject as "session not found".
297		if err := waitForSessionFileVisible(path, sessionFileVisibleTimeout); err != nil {
298			return "", fmt.Errorf("reconstructed %s session is not ready to resume: %w", plan.to.Name(), err)
299		}
300	
301		slog.Info("resume: wrote reconstructed session", "path", path, "newID", rec.SessionID)
302		fprintf(out, "\nReconstructed %s session into %s as %s.\n", plan.from.Name(), plan.to.Name(), shortID(rec.SessionID))
303		return rec.SessionID, nil
304	}
305	
306	// shortID abbreviates a session ID for display.
307	func shortID(id string) string {
308		if len(id) <= 13 {
309			return id
310		}
311		return id[:5] + "..." + id[len(id)-5:]
312	}
313	
314	// sessionFileVisibleTimeout bounds how long resume waits for a just-written reconstructed
315	// session file to become readable to other processes before launching the agent. It is
316	// generous enough to absorb the propagation lag of a weak-coherence filesystem (network or
317	// roaming home, cloud-synced folder, VM/container shared mount) yet short enough not to hang
318	// an interactive command — on a local disk the very first check passes, so it never waits.
319	const sessionFileVisibleTimeout = 10 * time.Second
320	
321	// writeReconstructedSession durably and atomically writes a reconstructed session file. A
322	// plain os.WriteFile leaves the data in the page cache and the new directory entry possibly
323	// non-durable; on a filesystem without strong close-to-open coherence the separately-spawned
324	// agent process can then fail to see a (large) file and report the session as missing. To
325	// avoid that, the content is written to a temp file in the SAME directory (so the rename is
326	// atomic rather than cross-device), fsync'd, renamed into place, and the parent directory is
327	// fsync'd so the entry itself is durable.
328	func writeReconstructedSession(path string, content []byte) (err error) {
329		dir := filepath.Dir(path)
330		if mkErr := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0o755); mkErr != nil {
331			return fmt.Errorf("failed to create target session directory: %w", mkErr)
332		}
333	
334		tmp, err := os.CreateTemp(dir, ".resume-*.tmp")
335		if err != nil {
336			return fmt.Errorf("failed to create temp session file: %w", err)
337		}
338		tmpPath := tmp.Name()
339		// Best-effort cleanup of the temp file on any early return; a no-op once it is renamed away.
340		defer func() { _ = os.Remove(tmpPath) }()
341	
342		// Preserve the world-readable mode the previous os.WriteFile(…, 0o644) produced
343		// (CreateTemp makes the file 0o600).
344		if chErr := tmp.Chmod(0o644); chErr != nil {
345			_ = tmp.Close()
346			return fmt.Errorf("failed to set session file mode: %w", chErr)
347		}
348		if _, wErr := tmp.Write(content); wErr != nil {
349			_ = tmp.Close()
350			return fmt.Errorf("failed to write temp session file: %w", wErr)
351		}
352		// Flush the bytes to the backing store before the rename so a reader cannot observe a
353		// renamed-but-empty file.
354		if syncErr := tmp.Sync(); syncErr != nil {
355			_ = tmp.Close()
356			return fmt.Errorf("failed to flush temp session file: %w", syncErr)
357		}
358		if closeErr := tmp.Close(); closeErr != nil {
359			return fmt.Errorf("failed to close temp session file: %w", closeErr)
360		}
361	
362		if rnErr := os.Rename(tmpPath, path); rnErr != nil {
363			return fmt.Errorf("failed to move session file into place: %w", rnErr)
364		}
365	
366		// Fsync the directory so the new entry is durable and propagates. Best-effort: not all
367		// platforms permit fsync on a directory handle, and it is a negligible cost on local disks.
368		if d, openErr := os.Open(dir); openErr == nil {
369			_ = d.Sync()
370			_ = d.Close()
371		}
372		return nil
373	}
374	
375	// waitForSessionFileVisible polls until path is readable — present AND with at least its
376	// first line available, since on a weak-coherence filesystem a stat can succeed before the
377	// content propagates — or the timeout elapses, in which case it returns a diagnostic error
378	// so the caller can fail clearly rather than launch a --resume the agent would reject.
379	func waitForSessionFileVisible(path string, timeout time.Duration) error {
380		deadline := time.Now().Add(timeout)
381		for {
382			readable, checkErr := sessionFileReadable(path)
383			if readable {
384				return nil
385			}
386			if time.Now().After(deadline) {
387				return fmt.Errorf("session file %q did not become readable within %s "+
388					"(the target filesystem may lack close-to-open coherence): %w", path, timeout, checkErr)
389			}
390			time.Sleep(50 * time.Millisecond)
391		}
392	}
393	
394	// sessionFileReadable reports whether path exists and at least its first line can be read.
395	// A reconstructed session always has at least one record, so an empty read means the content
396	// has not propagated yet even though the directory entry exists.
397	func sessionFileReadable(path string) (bool, error) {
398		f, err := os.Open(path)
399		if err != nil {
400			return false, err
401		}
402		defer func() { _ = f.Close() }()
403	
404		line, err := bufio.NewReader(f).ReadString('\n')
405		if len(line) > 0 {
406			return true, nil
407		}
408		if err == io.EOF || err == nil {
409			return false, fmt.Errorf("session file is empty")
410		}
411		return false, err
412	}
413
```

```text
1076	// resume plan (or nil if the user cancelled). On a successful selection it persists the

1152	// colorForAgent returns a stable accent color per provider for the list.
```

```text
415	// updateTarget handles the target-agent selection step.
416	func (m sessionTUI) updateTarget(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
417		switch msg.String() {
418		case "esc", "q":
419			m.mode = modeList
420			m.chosen = nil
421		case "ctrl+c":
422			m.result = sessionTUIResult{cancelled: true}
423			return m, tea.Quit
424		case "up", "k":
425			if m.targetCursor > 0 {
426				m.targetCursor--
427			}
428		case "down", "j":
429			if m.targetCursor < len(m.installed)-1 {
430				m.targetCursor++
431			}
432		case "enter":
433			m.result = sessionTUIResult{session: m.chosen, targetID: m.installed[m.targetCursor].id}
434			return m, tea.Quit
435		}
436		return m, nil
437	}
438	
439	// ---- list mechanics ----
440	
441	func (m *sessionTUI) moveCursor(delta int) {
442		if len(m.filtered) == 0 {
443			return
444		}
445		m.cursor += delta
446		if m.cursor < 0 {
447			m.cursor = 0
448		}
449		if m.cursor > len(m.filtered)-1 {
450			m.cursor = len(m.filtered) - 1
451		}
452		m.clampScroll()
453	}
454	
455	func (m *sessionTUI) clampScroll() {
456		h := m.listHeight()
457		if m.cursor < m.top {
458			m.top = m.cursor
459		}
460		if m.cursor >= m.top+h {
461			m.top = m.cursor - h + 1
462		}
463		if m.top < 0 {
464			m.top = 0
465		}
466	}
467	
468	func (m sessionTUI) selected() *sessionindex.Session {
469		if m.cursor < 0 || m.cursor >= len(m.filtered) {
470			return nil
471		}
472		return &m.filtered[m.cursor]
473	}
474	
475	// rowsPerSession is how many terminal lines one list row occupies in each view mode.
476	func (m sessionTUI) rowsPerSession() int {
477		if m.viewMode == "sparse" {
478			return 2
479		}
480		return 1
481	}
482	
483	// listHeight is how many sessions fit in the list region (height minus chrome).
484	func (m sessionTUI) listHeight() int {
485		const chrome = 6 // header(2) + glance(1) + footer(2) + margins
486		avail := m.height - chrome
487		if avail < 1 {
488			avail = 1
489		}
490		n := avail / m.rowsPerSession()
491		if n < 1 {
492			n = 1
493		}
494		return n
495	}
496	
497	func (m *sessionTUI) rebuildAgentCycle() {
498		present := map[string]bool{}
499		for _, s := range m.all {
500			present[s.Agent] = true
501		}
502		ids := make([]string, 0, len(present))
503		for id := range present {
504			ids = append(ids, id)
505		}
506		sort.Strings(ids)
507		m.agentCycle = append([]string{""}, ids...)
508	}
509	
510	func (m *sessionTUI) cycleAgent() tea.Cmd {
511		// In the all-projects search, cycle over every known agent and re-run the query; in a
512		// session list, cycle over the agents actually present and re-filter in place.
513		if m.mode == modeProjects && m.globalActive {
514			m.agentFilter = nextInCycle(m.allAgentCycle(), m.agentFilter)
515			m.searchSeq++
516			return searchDebounce(m.searchSeq, modeProjects)
517		}
518		m.agentFilter = nextInCycle(m.agentCycle, m.agentFilter)
519		m.applyFilter()
```

```text
592	func (m sessionTUI) defaultTargetIndex() int {
593		// Prefer the explicit preset (`resume <agent>`), else the last-resumed agent,
594		// else the chosen session's own agent (same-agent resume).
595		want := m.presetTo
596		if want == "" {
597			want = m.lastAgent
598		}
599		if want == "" && m.chosen != nil {
600			want = m.chosen.Agent
601		}
602		for i, a := range m.installed {
603			if a.id == want {
604				return i
605			}
606		}
607		return 0
608	}
609	
610	// ftsQuery turns free-form input into a safe FTS5 prefix query (alnum tokens only).
611	// minQueryLen is the shortest query we run a full-text search for. A single-character
612	// prefix (e.g. "t*") matches almost the entire corpus, and ranking + snippet generation
613	// over that set is pathologically slow (~80s on a ~800-session index), so we never fire
614	// it. Shared by resume and search so both behave the same.
615	const minQueryLen = 2
616	
617	// queryReady reports whether input has enough searchable characters (letters/digits, the
618	// same runes ftsQuery keeps) to run an FTS query. Below minQueryLen we don't search.
619	func queryReady(input string) bool {
620		n := 0
621		for _, r := range input {
622			if unicode.IsLetter(r) || unicode.IsDigit(r) {
623				n++
624				if n >= minQueryLen {
625					return true
626				}
627			}
628		}
629		return false
630	}
631	
632	// ftsQuery turns free-form input into an FTS5 query string. Three shapes:
633	//
634	//   - Bare words become independent prefix tokens, AND-ed (matched anywhere, any order) —
635	//     a loose search:  thank you      -> thank* you*
636	//   - A double-quoted span becomes a phrase: tokens adjacent and in order —
637	//     an exact phrase:  "thank you"    -> "thank you"
638	//   - A still-open quote (the user is mid-typing the phrase) keeps the final word a prefix,
639	//     so results keep updating live:  "thank yo    -> thank + yo*
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
338	// updateSearch handles the full-text search input. The typed character is applied to the
339	// input immediately (instant feedback); the FTS query runs async + debounced so a slow
340	// query never blocks typing. See searchDebounceMsg / searchResultMsg.
341	func (m sessionTUI) updateSearch(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
342		switch msg.String() {
343		case "esc":
344			m.searching = false
345			m.search.Blur()
346			m.searchQuery = ""
347			m.searchSeq++ // invalidate any in-flight search
348			m.snippetSeq++
349			m.applyFilter()
350			return m, nil
351		case "enter":
352			m.searching = false
353			m.search.Blur()
354			return m, nil
355		}
356		var cmd tea.Cmd
357		m.search, cmd = m.search.Update(msg)
358		m.searchQuery = m.search.Value()
359		m.searchSeq++
360		if !queryReady(m.searchQuery) {
361			// Too short to search: show the full (agent-filtered) list synchronously.
362			m.snippetSeq++
363			m.applyFilter()
364			return m, cmd
365		}
366		return m, tea.Batch(cmd, searchDebounce(m.searchSeq, modeList))
367	}

372	func (m sessionTUI) updatePreview(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
```

```text
100	func GetRegistry() *Registry {
101		registry.ensureInitialized()
102		return registry

104	
105	// Get retrieves a provider by ID (case-insensitive)
106	func (r *Registry) Get(id string) (spi.Provider, error) {
107		r.ensureInitialized()
108		r.mu.RLock()
109		defer r.mu.RUnlock()
110	
111		// Case-insensitive lookup
112		for provID, provider := range r.providers {
113			if strings.EqualFold(provID, id) {
114				slog.Debug("Retrieved provider", "requested_id", id, "matched_id", provID, "name", provider.Name())
115				return provider, nil
116			}
117		}
118	
119		// Log available providers when lookup fails to aid debugging
120		availableIDs := make([]string, 0, len(r.providers))
121		for id := range r.providers {
122			availableIDs = append(availableIDs, id)
123		}
124		sort.Strings(availableIDs)
125	
126		slog.Warn("Provider not found",
127			"requested_id", id,
128			"available_providers", availableIDs)
129	
130		return nil, fmt.Errorf("provider '%s' not found", id)
131	}
132	
133	// GetAll returns all registered providers as a map of ID to Provider
134	func (r *Registry) GetAll() map[string]spi.Provider {
135		r.ensureInitialized()
136		r.mu.RLock()
137		defer r.mu.RUnlock()
138	
139		// Return a copy to prevent external modification
140		result := make(map[string]spi.Provider)
141		for id, provider := range r.providers {
142			result[id] = provider
143		}
144	
145		return result
146	}
147	
148	// ListIDs returns all registered provider IDs (sorted)
149	func (r *Registry) ListIDs() []string {
150		r.ensureInitialized()
151		r.mu.RLock()
152		defer r.mu.RUnlock()
153	
154		ids := make([]string, 0, len(r.providers))
155		for id := range r.providers {
156			ids = append(ids, id)
157		}
158	
159		sort.Strings(ids)
160		return ids
161	}
162	
163	// GetDefault returns the default provider (Claude)
164	func (r *Registry) GetDefault() (spi.Provider, error) {
165		r.ensureInitialized()
166		slog.Debug("Getting default provider (claude)")
167		return r.Get("claude")
168	}
```

```text
645	func (m sessionTUI) defaultTargetIndex() int {
646		// Prefer the explicit preset (`resume <agent>`), else the last-resumed agent,
647		// else the chosen session's own agent (same-agent resume).
648		want := m.presetTo
649		if want == "" {
650			want = m.lastAgent
651		}
652		if want == "" && m.chosen != nil {
653			want = m.chosen.Agent
654		}
655		for i, a := range m.installed {
656			if a.id == want {
657				return i
658			}
659		}
660		return 0
661	}
```

```text
15	func TestFTSQuery(t *testing.T) {
16		cases := []struct{ name, in, want string }{
17			{"empty", "", ""},
18			{"single word is a prefix", "thank", "thank*"},
19			{"bare words are independent prefixes", "thank you", "thank* you*"},
20			{"punctuation is stripped from bare words", "max-cpu!", "maxcpu*"},
21			{"closed phrase is exact adjacency", `"thank you"`, `"thank you"`},
22			{"closed single-word phrase has no prefix", `"thank"`, `"thank"`},
23			{"open phrase keeps last word a prefix", `"thank yo`, "thank + yo*"},
24			{"open single-word phrase is a prefix", `"thank`, "thank*"},
25			{"phrase plus trailing bare word", `"thank you" now`, `"thank you" now*`},
26			{"bare word before a closed phrase", `please "thank you"`, `please* "thank you"`},
27			{"whitespace inside a phrase is collapsed", `"thank    you"`, `"thank you"`},
28		}
29		for _, c := range cases {
30			t.Run(c.name, func(t *testing.T) {
31				if got := ftsQuery(c.in); got != c.want {
32					t.Errorf("ftsQuery(%q) = %q, want %q", c.in, got, c.want)
33				}
34			})
35		}
36	}
37	
38	func TestQueryReady(t *testing.T) {
39		cases := []struct {
40			in   string
41			want bool
42		}{
43			{"", false},
44			{"a", false},   // one alnum char is below minQueryLen
45			{`"a"`, false}, // quotes don't count toward the threshold
46			{"ab", true},   // two alnum chars
47			{"a!", false},  // punctuation doesn't count
48			{"a b", true},  // two alnum chars across words
49			{`"hi"`, true}, // alnum inside quotes counts
50			{"日本", true},   // letters in other scripts count
51		}
52		for _, c := range cases {
53			if got := queryReady(c.in); got != c.want {
54				t.Errorf("queryReady(%q) = %v, want %v", c.in, got, c.want)
55			}
56		}
57	}
58	
59	func TestShortID(t *testing.T) {
60		cases := []struct{ in, want string }{
61			{"short", "short"},
62			{"1234567890abc", "1234567890abc"}, // <= 13 chars: unchanged
63			{"1234567890abcdef", "12345...bcdef"},
64		}
65		for _, c := range cases {
66			if got := shortID(c.in); got != c.want {
67				t.Errorf("shortID(%q) = %q, want %q", c.in, got, c.want)
68			}
69		}
70	}

72	func TestWriteReconstructedSession(t *testing.T) {
73		dir := t.TempDir()
74		path := filepath.Join(dir, "sub", "session.jsonl")
75		content := []byte(`{"type":"user"}` + "\n")
76	
77		if err := writeReconstructedSession(path, content); err != nil {
78			t.Fatalf("writeReconstructedSession: %v", err)
79		}
80	
81		got, err := os.ReadFile(path)
82		if err != nil {
83			t.Fatalf("reading written file: %v", err)
84		}
85		if string(got) != string(content) {
86			t.Errorf("content = %q, want %q", got, content)
87		}
88

92			t.Fatal(err)
93		}

96				t.Errorf("unexpected leftover file in target dir: %q", e.Name())
97			}
98		}

124	}
125	
126	func TestWaitForSessionFileVisible(t *testing.T) {
127		dir := t.TempDir()
128	
129		present := filepath.Join(dir, "present.jsonl")
130		if err := os.WriteFile(present, []byte(`{"type":"user"}`+"\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
131			t.Fatal(err)
132		}
133		if err := waitForSessionFileVisible(present, time.Second); err != nil {
134			t.Errorf("expected an already-present file to be visible: %v", err)
135		}
136	
137		// A file that never appears must time out with a diagnostic error rather than block forever.
138		if err := waitForSessionFileVisible(filepath.Join(dir, "never.jsonl"), 100*time.Millisecond); err == nil {
139			t.Error("expected timeout error for a file that never becomes visible")
140		}
141	}

186	// TestPrepareResumeTargetLoadsSourceFromOriginCwd guards the cross-project resume fix:
187	// the source session must be loaded from the directory it was launched in (fromCwd),
188	// not the user's current cwd, while the reconstructed file is still written under the
189	// current cwd. Regression test for "source session ... has no data to reconstruct" when
190	// resuming a session picked from another project via the all-projects browser.
191	func TestPrepareResumeTargetLoadsSourceFromOriginCwd(t *testing.T) {
192		nativeDir := t.TempDir()
193		from := &fakeProvider{name: "Codex CLI"}
194		to := &fakeProvider{name: "Claude Code", nativeDir: nativeDir}
195	
196		const originCwd = "/Users/jake/dev/tmp/blog-site"
197		const currentCwd = "/Users/jake/dev/specstory-website"
198	
199		plan := &resumePlan{
200			from:      from,
201			fromID:    "codex",
202			sessionID: "019c4cdd-917a-74e3-9b2e-fdb45e9eddc5",
203			fromCwd:   originCwd,
204			to:        to,
205			toID:      "claude",
206		}
207	
208		newID, err := prepareResumeTarget(plan, currentCwd, io.Discard)
209		if err != nil {
210			t.Fatalf("prepareResumeTarget returned error: %v", err)
211		}
212		if newID != "new-session-id" {
213			t.Errorf("resume target id = %q, want %q", newID, "new-session-id")
214		}
215		if from.gotLoadPath != originCwd {
216			t.Errorf("source loaded from %q, want origin cwd %q (current cwd %q must not be used)",
217				from.gotLoadPath, originCwd, currentCwd)
218		}
219	}
220	
221	// TestPrepareResumeTargetFallsBackToCurrentCwd verifies that when the index row carries
222	// no origin cwd (older rows), the source load falls back to the current cwd rather than
223	// loading from an empty path.
224	func TestPrepareResumeTargetFallsBackToCurrentCwd(t *testing.T) {
225		nativeDir := t.TempDir()

236			to:        to,

242		}
243		if from.gotLoadPath != currentCwd {
244			t.Errorf("source loaded from %q, want fallback to current cwd %q", from.gotLoadPath, currentCwd)
245		}
246	}
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-24 20:30:19Z)**_

All green. Here's a summary of what I changed and why.

## The problem

`specstory resume [agent]` accepted an optional target-agent argument, but it only set the *default cursor position* in the interactive target-selection step — the user was still prompted to pick a target. And an invalid agent name was silently ignored (it fell through to the first installed agent).

## The fix

Two behavior changes, split across the natural boundaries of the code:

**1. Validate the argument (`pkg/cmd/resume.go`)**

When the arg is provided, it's now checked against the provider registry up front. An unknown name fails the run with a `ValidationError` listing the valid agents, rather than being ignored:

```
Unknown agent "bogusagent". Valid agents: claude (Claude Code), codex (Codex CLI), ...
```

Exit code is 1. I did the *known-provider* check here (where the registry is in scope), and deferred the *is-it-installed* check to where the installed set is actually computed.

**2. Skip the prompt when a valid agent is given (`pkg/cmd/session_tui.go`)**

- In `selectResumeViaTUI`, once the installed set is known, a preset target is validated against it (erroring if the agent is known but not installed) and resolved to its canonical ID.
- Added a `beginResume(sess)` helper that is the single entry point from "session chosen" to "launch." When a preset target is set, it records the result and quits immediately — the target-selection step is never shown. Otherwise it advances to that step as before.
- Routed all three "resume this session" call sites (list `r`, preview `r`, global-search `r`) through `beginResume`, replacing the duplicated inline `m.mode = modeTarget` logic.
- Simplified `defaultTargetIndex` since the preset branch can no longer reach it, and updated the help text.

## Verification

- `go build` clean, `golangci-lint run` reports 0 issues, full `go test ./...` passes.
- Added two focused tests (`TestBeginResumeWithPresetSkipsTargetStep`, `TestBeginResumeWithoutPresetEntersTargetStep`) covering both branches of the new logic.
- Manually confirmed `resume bogusagent` errors with exit 1.

One design note worth flagging: `search`'s resume path passes no preset, so it's unaffected — it still routes through the same `beginResume` and shows the target step as before.

---

```text
func DefaultErrorHandler(w io.Writer, styles Styles, err error) {
	if w, ok := w.(term.File); ok {
		// if stderr is not a tty, simply print the error without any
		// styling or going through an [ErrorHandler]:
		if !term.IsTerminal(w.Fd()) {
			_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(w, err.Error())
			return
		}
	}
	_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(w, styles.ErrorHeader.String())
	_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(w, styles.ErrorText.Render(err.Error()+"."))
	_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(w)
	if isUsageError(err) {
		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(w, lipgloss.JoinHorizontal(
			lipgloss.Left,
			styles.ErrorText.UnsetWidth().Render("Try"),
			styles.Program.Flag.Render(" --help "),
			styles.ErrorText.UnsetWidth().UnsetMargins().UnsetTransform().Render("for usage."),
		))
		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(w)
	}
}
```

```text
func titleFirstWord(s string) string {
	words := strings.Fields(s)
	if len(words) == 0 {
		return s
	}
	words[0] = cases.Title(language.AmericanEnglish).String(words[0])
	return strings.Join(words, " ")
}
```

```text
672	// ftsQuery turns free-form input into a safe FTS5 prefix query (alnum tokens only).
673	// minQueryLen is the shortest query we run a full-text search for. A single-character
674	// prefix (e.g. "t*") matches almost the entire corpus, and ranking + snippet generation
675	// over that set is pathologically slow (~80s on a ~800-session index), so we never fire
676	// it. Shared by resume and search so both behave the same.
677	const minQueryLen = 2
678	
679	// queryReady reports whether input has enough searchable characters (letters/digits, the
680	// same runes ftsQuery keeps) to run an FTS query. Below minQueryLen we don't search.
681	func queryReady(input string) bool {
682		n := 0
683		for _, r := range input {
684			if unicode.IsLetter(r) || unicode.IsDigit(r) {
685				n++
686				if n >= minQueryLen {
687					return true
688				}
689			}
690		}
691		return false
692	}
693	
694	// ftsQuery turns free-form input into an FTS5 query string. Three shapes:
695	//
696	//   - Bare words become independent prefix tokens, AND-ed (matched anywhere, any order) —
697	//     a loose search:  thank you      -> thank* you*
698	//   - A double-quoted span becomes a phrase: tokens adjacent and in order —
699	//     an exact phrase:  "thank you"    -> "thank you"
700	//   - A still-open quote (the user is mid-typing the phrase) keeps the final word a prefix,
701	//     so results keep updating live:  "thank yo    -> thank + yo*
702	//
703	// Only letters and digits survive tokenization, so no FTS5 syntax character from the raw
704	// input can reach MATCH — every query this builds is safe to execute.
705	func ftsQuery(input string) string {
706		// Splitting on '"' yields alternating runs: even indexes are outside quotes, odd indexes
707		// are inside. An odd-indexed run that is also the LAST run means the closing quote hasn't
708		// been typed yet (an open phrase), so its final word stays a prefix for live type-ahead.
709		segs := strings.Split(input, `"`)
710		var parts []string
711		for i, seg := range segs {
712			words := alnumWords(seg)
713			if len(words) == 0 {
714				continue
715			}
716			switch {
717			case i%2 == 0: // outside quotes: loose prefix tokens
718				for _, w := range words {
719					parts = append(parts, w+"*")
720				}
721			case i == len(segs)-1: // open phrase (still typing): adjacency + prefix last token
722				parts = append(parts, strings.Join(words, " + ")+"*")
723			default: // closed phrase: exact adjacency
724				parts = append(parts, `"`+strings.Join(words, " ")+`"`)
725			}
726		}
727		return strings.Join(parts, " ")
728	}
729	
730	// alnumWords splits s on whitespace and reduces each field to its letters and digits,
731	// dropping any field with none. This is the tokenization FTS5's default tokenizer applies,
732	// mirrored here so queryReady and ftsQuery agree on what counts as a searchable token.
733	func alnumWords(s string) []string {
734		var words []string
735		for _, f := range strings.Fields(s) {
736			var b strings.Builder
737			for _, r := range f {
738				if unicode.IsLetter(r) || unicode.IsDigit(r) {
739					b.WriteRune(r)
740				}
741			}
742			if b.Len() > 0 {
743				words = append(words, b.String())
744			}
745		}
746		return words
747	}
748

753		styFaint  = lipgloss.NewStyle().Faint(true)
754		styBold   = lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true)
755		styCursor = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("213")).Bold(true)
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-25 22:36:18Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-25 22:36:37Z)**_

I have a complete picture. Here's the diagnosis.

## What's happening

It's a **tokenization mismatch between how the body is indexed and how your query is built**. The two sides disagree on what `poem.txt` is.

**Index side** — `sessions_fts` (`pkg/sessionindex/store.go:162`) is created with no `tokenize=` clause, so it uses FTS5's default **`unicode61`** tokenizer. That tokenizer treats every non-alphanumeric character — including `.` — as a *separator*. So when a session body contains `poem.txt`, it's indexed as **two adjacent tokens**: `poem` and `txt`. There is no token `poemtxt` anywhere in the index.

**Query side** — `ftsQuery` → `alnumWords` (`pkg/cmd/session_tui.go:733`) tokenizes differently. It splits only on **whitespace**, then within each field it *deletes* non-alphanumerics and concatenates what's left:

```
"poem.txt"  --Fields-->  ["poem.txt"]  --strip '.'-->  "poemtxt"
```

So your query becomes `poemtxt*` (or, quoted, the phrase `"poemtxt"`). That asks the index for a token starting with `poemtxt` — which doesn't exist — so you get zero hits.

Meanwhile:
- `poem` → `poem*` → matches the `poem` token ✅
- `txt` → `txt*` → matches the `txt` token ✅
- `poem.txt` → `poemtxt*` → matches nothing ❌

Quoting doesn't help because the quoted path runs through the same `alnumWords`, so `"poem.txt"` also collapses to the single bogus token `poemtxt`.

The existing test actually **encodes this bug** as if it were intended: `pkg/cmd/resume_test.go:21` asserts `"max-cpu!" → "maxcpu*"`. That query is equally broken — the body indexes `max-cpu` as `max` + `cpu`, so `maxcpu*` can never match it.

## The root cause in one sentence

`alnumWords` removes intra-word punctuation and fuses the pieces into one token, but `unicode61` splits on that same punctuation into multiple tokens — so any query term containing punctuation produces a token the index will never contain.

## How we might fix it

The principle: **the query tokenizer must split on the same boundaries unicode61 does** (any non-alphanumeric), not just whitespace. Then `poem.txt` yields two tokens, matching the index. Two reasonable shapes for the result:

1. **Loose (AND, any order)** — `poem.txt` → `poem* txt*`. Simplest; consistent with how multi-word bare input already behaves. Slightly looser than the user might expect (the two tokens need only appear *somewhere* in the session, not together).

2. **Adjacency phrase (my recommendation)** — since `poem` and `txt` were contiguous in the input (only punctuation between them), treat them as a phrase: `"poem txt"` (or `poem + txt*` while still typing, for live prefix). Because unicode61 indexes them at adjacent positions, an FTS5 phrase query matches `poem.txt` occurrences *precisely* — and won't spuriously match a session that mentions "poem" in one place and "txt" in another. This best reflects what the user typed.

Implementation would be a change to `alnumWords` (split each field on non-alphanumeric runs instead of stripping them) plus a small decision in `ftsQuery` about whether sub-tokens from one input word become loose tokens or an adjacency phrase. The `TestFTSQuery`/`TestQueryReady` cases (notably the `max-cpu!` one) would need updating to reflect the corrected behavior.

A couple of things to decide before implementing:
- **Bare multi-punctuation words** like `a.b.c` → phrase `"a b c"` vs loose `a* b* c*`.
- Whether the **prefix `*`** still belongs on the last sub-token in committed (closed) searches — for an exact filename match you may *not* want `poem txt` to prefix-match `txt` into `txture`, etc.

One more note: no reindex is needed for the fix — the index already stores the right tokens (`poem`, `txt`). This is purely a query-construction bug, so fixing `ftsQuery`/`alnumWords` is sufficient.

```bash
}
```

Proven end-to-end against the exact FTS5 engine the project uses (`modernc.org/sqlite`):

| Query | Source input | Matches `…poem.txt…` |
|---|---|---|
| `poemtxt*` | **old** behavior | **0** ❌ (the bug) |
| `poem + txt*` | new — bare `poem.txt` | **1** ✅ |
| `"poem txt"` | new — quoted `"poem.txt"` | **1** ✅ |
| `poem*` / `txt*` | partial searches | 1 each ✅ (already worked) |

## What I changed

**The fix is entirely query-side** (`pkg/cmd/session_tui.go`) — no reindex needed, since the index already stored the right tokens (`poem`, `txt`).

1. **Replaced `alnumWords` with `fieldTokens`** — instead of *stripping* punctuation and fusing a word into one token (`poem.txt` → `poemtxt`), it now *splits* on every non-alphanumeric run, exactly like the index's `unicode61` tokenizer (`poem.txt` → `poem`, `txt`). Added `segmentTokens` to flatten a whole quoted segment the same way.

2. **`ftsQuery` builds adjacency phrases** for any word that tokenizes into more than one token, honoring your prefix rule:
   - Bare `poem.txt` → `poem + txt*` (adjacent, prefix the last token for live type-ahead)
   - Open quote `"poem.txt` → `poem + txt*` (still typing → prefix)
   - Closed quote `"poem.txt"` → `"poem txt"` (committed phrase → **no** prefix)

Single-token words and the existing loose/phrase shapes are unchanged, so `thank you → thank* you*`, `"thank you" → "thank you"`, etc. all still hold.

## Tests

Updated `TestFTSQuery`: the old `"max-cpu!" → "maxcpu*"` case (which encoded the bug) is now `"max-cpu!" → "max + cpu*"`, plus three new filename cases covering bare / closed-quote / open-quote. Full `pkg/cmd` and `pkg/sessionindex` suites pass; `golangci-lint` clean.

One side effect worth noting: searches like `max-cpu` now require `max` and `cpu` to be **adjacent** (a phrase), where before they matched a nonexistent fused token (i.e. matched nothing). That's strictly more correct, but it is a behavior change for any punctuation-joined query — they're now precise adjacency matches rather than loose AND. If you'd ever want punctuation-joined terms to be loose-AND instead of adjacent, that'd be a one-line change in the outside-quotes branch, but adjacency is what you asked for and it's the faithful interpretation of a typed filename.
